sections.paragraph: m. Lev 11:18, and רָחָֽמָה (Milêl), Deu 14:17, a smaller kind of vulture, white, with black wings, feeding on dead bodies; vultur percnopterus, Linn.; so called from its affection towards its young, like חֲסִירָה stork; Arab. رخم and رخمة. See Bochart, Hieroz. t. ii, p. 297-322.
